commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is required because this maintainer/protected PR's remaining blocker is secops acceptance of a new branch-write credential boundary, not a narrow automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally adds a new contents-write GitHub App token path in pull_request_target, so it needs explicit security-owner acceptance before merge. (sha=c860526af87531d8dee829def75eb599b9788778)
commands.review_summary:
The PR splits Dependency Guard into detect, autoscrub, and enforce jobs, then adds script and test support to remove package lockfile-only diffs with scoped GitHub App GraphQL cleanup commits.

PR surface: Tests +358, Config +76, Other +329. Total +763 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a CI automation feature, not a bug report. Source inspection shows current main lacks an autoscrub path and the PR adds one.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is required because this maintainer/protected PR's remaining blocker is secops acceptance of a new branch-write credential boundary, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(ci): autoscrub dependency lockfile-only PR changes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds deterministic autoscrubbing for package lockfile-only PR changes when the PR branch is in the main OpenClaw repository or a fork branch with maintainer edits enabled.
- Restores only changed package lockfiles from the PR base tree, then creates one narrow cleanup commit: `chore: remove dependency lockfile change`.
- Uses a short-lived GitHub App token only for the autoscrub branch cleanup commit and commits through GitHub GraphQL `createCommitOnBranch`, so autoscrubbed heads are real PR head updates instead of `GITHUB_TOKEN` ref writes.
- Leaves unpushable fork PRs, mixed dependency-related changes, and GitHub write failures blocked with deterministic comments that explain the exact manual next step.
- Skips autoscrub when any changed `package.json` modifies dependency graph fields, or when dependency-related non-lockfile files such as `pnpm-workspace.yaml` or `patches/**` are also changed.

## User-Facing Comments

This PR adds one success comment and expands the blocked comment:

- `Dependency lockfile changes were removed`: posted after a successful autoscrub commit, listing restored lockfiles, target branch, and cleanup commit SHA.
- `Dependency graph changes are blocked`: explains whether autoscrub was not attempted for an unpushable branch, skipped because manifest dependency graph fields changed, skipped because non-lockfile dependency files also changed, or attempted but rejected by GitHub.

## Security / Automation Notes

- The workflow keeps using trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`; it does not check out or execute PR-head code.
- The default workflow permissions keep `contents: read`; the autoscrub job uses a head-repository-scoped GitHub App token for the deterministic branch cleanup commit while comments and labels remain authored by `github-actions[bot]`.
- The final required check remains named `dependency-guard` and reruns the trusted script in enforce mode after the optional autoscrub job.
- Autoscrub is limited to PR heads with a concrete ref and SHA where maintainers can modify the branch: same-repository branches or fork branches with maintainer edits enabled.
- Fork autoscrub scopes the App token request to the PR head owner/repository, writes through `createCommitOnBranch`, and falls back to the blocked comment if GitHub rejects token creation or the cleanup commit.
- Dependency graph changes remain blocked unless the current head SHA is explicitly authorized through the existing secops override flow.

## Verification

- `node --check scripts/github/dependency-guard.mjs`
- YAML parse for `.github/workflows/dependency-guard.yml`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Direct Node assertions for same-repository autoscrub, fork autoscrub denial, maintainer-editable fork autoscrub, lockfile-only eligibility, and mixed lockfile plus `patches/**` / `pnpm-workspace.yaml` denial
- Configured App-token owner/repository outputs from dependency guard detect mode and trusted marker-comment author assertions
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-guard-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-guard-script.test.ts`
target.author: romneyda
